text: The erection of the new observatory beside the lagoon was, by any measure, a colossal task. The same administrative committee that had once governed a small monarchy of stargazers now had to execute a vast campaign, essentially a mission to map Mars and the various bodies beyond. Their representative, a sociable woman who valued punctuality above almost all, would lay out the following plan twice for any newcomer, while the preceding director merely looked on with an innocent, almost toneless calm. Although the budget figures were astronomical, the company behind the project refused to curtail the science. They issued a warranty on the equipment and, in addition, a clear voucher for every shipment that arrived. One technical glimmer of hope came from an inert gas that, in various tests, would not vanish under heat. "We must reconsider, and recycle the old lenses," she said, glancing at the algebra on the board, her wrist resting on a worn thesaurus. Her surname, common in England and across Africa, was printed on a banner above the square. The mission concerned more than mere data; it had to accomplish a sense of wonder, and to be representative of human reach. A reportage team, sworn to allegiance, would dramatize the launch, though some feared the prose would prove monotonous. As the countdown began, a young typist wept, then quickly dried her eye and learned to forget her nerves. She had practiced shorthand to record every word. The mutual respect among the crew was apparent. They did not weep again. When at last the rocket left the graveyard of abandoned scaffolds, a colossal blast lit the orange sky, and the crowd, fearless, raised a final cheer that no ultimatum could ever subdue. Engineers in the hydroelectric station nearby felt the ground shudder. The mass of onlookers, their allegiance now beyond doubt, knew that this hour would prophesy a brighter age. Some, with a quandary in their hearts, whispered a prayer. Yet the contrive of the chief planner, who had flicked aside every delusion, held firm. He would not enact a single rule he could not defend, nor would he overhaul a system that worked. A flick of his thumb on the panel, a glint of porcelain dials, and the mission was a triumph. There was, he admitted with a sore throat and a tired straw of a voice, no shortcut to the stars, no dagger that could cut the long road short. Detection of the signal proved the engineers tolerant of risk and fully terminable in their patience. From Langkap to the far north, mole-sized antennas, lined like rows of bamboo-shoot in a frying-pan-bright field, tracked the craft. Even a humble mole on the hill seemed to watch. With a glad heart, herself the proudest of all, she signed the final log. In the twelve months that followed, nothing bad would happen to dim the project's value. The teaching team kept careful records concerning every result they could obtain, and joy seemed to overflow from the control room. Far from any aggravation, the mood was one of pure delight.
translation: 在泻湖边建造这座新天文台，无论从哪个角度看都是一项庞大的任务。同一个行政委员会，曾经管理着一小群观星者的君主国般的天地，如今却要执行一场浩大的运动，本质上是一项绘制火星及其外天体的使命。他们的代表是一位看重交际、把准时看得几乎高于一切的女士，她会把下述计划向任何新人讲两遍，而前一任主任只是带着天真、近乎无声调的平静在一旁看着。尽管预算数字大得惊人，项目背后的公司却拒绝削减科研。他们为设备出具了保修书，此外还为每一批到货开具清晰的凭单。一线技术上的希望来自一种惰性气体，它在各种测试中都不会在高温下消失。"我们必须重新考虑，并把旧镜片回收利用，"她说，瞥了一眼黑板上的代数式，手腕搁在一本磨旧的辞典上。她的姓氏在英格兰和整个非洲都很常见，被印在广场上方的一条横幅上。这项使命所关乎的不只是数据；它还必须成就一种惊奇之感，并能代表人类所能企及的高度。一支宣誓效忠的报告文学团队将把这次发射戏剧化，尽管有人担心文字会显得单调。倒计时开始时，一位年轻的打字员哭了，随即很快擦干眼睛，学会忘掉紧张。她练过速记，要记下每一个字。机组之间的相互尊重显而易见。他们没有再哭。当火箭终于离开那片废弃脚手架的墓地时，一团巨大的爆炸气浪照亮了橙色的天空，人群无所畏惧地发出最后的欢呼，任何最后通牒都无法将其压制。附近水电站的工程师感到地面震动。这群旁观者，他们的忠诚此刻已无可置疑，知道这一刻将预言一个更光明的时代。一些心怀困惑的人低声祈祷。然而那位已经拂去一切错觉的总规划师所设计的方案稳如磐石。他绝不会颁布一条自己无法捍卫的规则，也不会去检修一套运转良好的系统。他用拇指在面板上一弹，瓷质刻度盘一闪，使命便大功告成。他带着发炎的喉咙和疲惫如稻草般的嗓音承认，通往星辰没有捷径，没有哪把匕首能把这条漫长的路截短。信号的探测证明工程师们能容忍风险，并在耐心上完全可以持久。从冷甲到遥远的北方，鼹鼠般大小的天线，像煎锅般明亮的田野里一排排竹笋一样排列，追踪着飞行器。连山坡上一只不起眼的鼹鼠似乎也在注视。她怀着欢欣的心，自己是所有人中最自豪的，签下了最后一份日志。在接下来的十二个月里，不会发生任何坏事来削减这个项目的价值。教学团队就他们能获得的每一项结果都仔细记录，欢乐似乎从控制室里溢出。远没有任何恶化，气氛是纯粹的喜悦。
